evals.prompt: In this nix-config repo, update only the nixpkgs flake input to latest. Keep the lock diff scoped, don't update everything, and tell me how you verified it.
evals.expected_output: A scoped nixpkgs update plan or implementation that avoids broad lock churn and verifies the result.
expectations: Uses or recommends a scoped command like `nix flake update nixpkgs`, not plain `nix flake update`.
Inspects or plans to inspect `flake.lock` / `git diff` for unrelated input movement.
Includes verification with `nix flake metadata`, `nix flake check --no-build`, full `nix flake check`, or a targeted build/eval.

evals.prompt: Update the Hyprland-related flake inputs. Don't just check the newest release note; look at the full delta from the currently pinned version and adjust config if needed.
evals.expected_output: A Hyprland ecosystem update workflow that researches release notes across the full version range and applies required config migrations without optional churn.
expectations: Compares current adopted version/revision to target and reviews the full release-note range, not only the latest patch note.
Identifies required config migrations separately from optional new features.
Preserves compatibility/follows relationships for related Hyprland ecosystem inputs unless there is evidence to change them.

evals.prompt: Bump two related inputs: my-overlay and my-plugin. my-plugin follows my-overlay's nixpkgs. Make sure the update doesn't accidentally duplicate nixpkgs pins or break ABI compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A related-input update workflow that checks follows/shared dependencies and compatibility before and after the scoped update.
expectations: Explicitly checks and preserves `inputs.<name>.follows` relationships when they express compatibility or deduplication.
Uses a scoped multi-input update command rather than updating every flake input.
Reviews lock metadata/diff to confirm expected direct and transitive movement only.

evals.prompt: After updating one flake input, `nix flake check` fails in a totally different module. Decide what to do next; don't just assume the update broke it.
evals.expected_output: A failure-triage workflow that isolates whether the failure is caused by the update or pre-existing/unrelated.
expectations: Uses targeted eval/build commands for the updated input or affected output before blaming the update.
Suggests comparing against the pre-update `HEAD` or baseline state when the failure looks unrelated.
Reports unrelated/pre-existing failures only with concrete evidence from commands or diffs.
